summary: Loader stub pulls unrelated scoped dependency at require time
details: @node-cloud/create@1.0.5 advertises itself as 'cloud definitions for node' but ships an index.js that is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer polyfill, with a single added top-level line `var ins = require('@sql-trigger/nodesql');`. The variable `ins` is never referenced; the require's only effect is to force load-time evaluation of the unrelated @sql-trigger/nodesql package in any consumer that imports @node-cloud/create. No install lifecycle scripts are declared, so the side effect fires at require/import time rather than on `npm install`. The package metadata, README stub, and shipped code body are inconsistent with each other, consistent with a loader-stub whose function is to drag a separately-published dependency into installer trees. Whether actual installer harm occurs depends on the contents of @sql-trigger/nodesql, which must be assessed separately.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
